hypothesis: Iter 77 = HONEST SUBSTRATE + LEVEL ANCHORS. Two changes, one launched mechanism under test. (A) HONEST BENCHMARK (measurement only, cannot move training): the eval was scoring a near-flat window — over the real-user scored window (t=510-690) glucose moves only ~2.9 mg/dL, hr ~3 bpm, so MAPE rewarded 'land a calibrated constant', not 'predict dynamics'; persistence (carry the last calibration reading forward) already scores ~0.014 glucose MAPE there. benchmark.py now reports (1) skill_vs_persistence per marker (1=perfect, 0=no better than persistence, <0=worse), (2) a real-vs-teacher source split so the circular cold-model-cohort numbers (hormones scored against the very teacher the net distils from) never enter the measured-data headline, and (3) default_thresholds() now LOADS benchmark.thresholds.json instead of a drifted hand-copy — re-enabling the textbook gate (0.0 -> 0.45) that had been silently disabled locally, the one fit-free non-circular dynamics check. The gate's pass/fail field is unchanged (still mixed overall_weighted_mape); only honest VIEWS were added, so this cannot regress the gate. (B) LEVEL ANCHORS (the mechanism under test): insulin was the worst marker (~0.90) for a structural reason — every STRONG insulin signal is offset/level-blind by construction (insulin_sweep w=0.30 supervises dI/dt teacher-forced, never integrated; dose_response w=0.40 supervises Δpeak above the model's own free baseline) and insulin was EXCLUDED from cold-distill, so its absolute level rested only on ~3 cohort specs at effective weight ~2.6e-3. Same disease for lactate (insulin_sweep species-weight 0.0; only a delta cohort + zero-embedding default-baseline) and hepatic_output (sweep rate + one delta). FIX: add insulin, lactate, hepatic_output to the cold-distill ANCHORED set (mode already anchored from iter 76), which pins each marker's absolute LEVEL via the short free-rollout depth anchor — the level authority these markers never had. All three are simulated in the cold ODE (indices 1/5/6), so the reference trajectory exists. Attribution note: the optimizer-honesty change (auxiliary-signal gradient accumulation — the ~10 constraint signals each take a solo clipped step that erases their weight and fights the others; trajectory's per-window SGD is fine and stays) is DEFERRED to iter 78, to be designed and retuned against this iter's honest baseline rather than launched blind.
expectedEffect: Primary (vs the honest metric this run itself emits): insulin per-marker mean_mape drops materially from the ~0.90 standing gap as its level anchor engages; watch dist_insulin in the cold_model_distillation telemetry (should be finite and fall over phase 2) and insulin's persistence skill turn less-negative / positive. Secondary: lactate and hepatic_output levels stop floating (dist_lactate / dist_hepatic_output finite and falling). Honest-benchmark reads to record as the new baseline for iter 78: per_marker_by_source['real'] skill_vs_persistence for glucose/hr/sbp/dbp/temp (expect glucose skill to be a HARD bar — persistence ~0.014 — so near-zero-to-negative is the honest truth, not a regression), overall_weighted_mape_by_source split real vs teacher, and textbook_mean_pass_rate now that its gate is live. Risks: (R1) anchoring insulin could fight insulin_sweep's rate signal — they are complementary (level vs rate) but watch for insulin oscillation; if it regresses, drop insulin from the anchor set first. (R2) three extra anchored markers add modest phase-2 cost (they ride the same per-protocol rollouts; markers are just scored on them) — anchor_skips and OOM unlikely but monitored. (R3) textbook gate now live at 0.45 — if textbook_mean_pass_rate sits below 0.45 the gate fails; that is the gate doing its job (the check was disabled before), not a code bug.
